./.cursor/plans/Context Token Optimization-48187ddf.plan.md:  - id: "disable-rtk-claude"
./.cursor/plans/Context Token Optimization-48187ddf.plan.md:**Cursor only** — Use `rtk` prefix variants: `rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo clippy`.
./.cursor/plans/Context Token Optimization-48187ddf.plan.md:**Claude Code** — Do NOT use `rtk` prefix. context-mode handles output
./.cursor/plans/Context Token Optimization-48187ddf.plan.md:- **RTK muscle memory**: Developers accustomed to `rtk cargo build` will need to drop the prefix in Claude Code sessions. Add a lint or reminder in AGENTS.md.
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:  - id: "add-rtk-section"
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:    content: "Add @RTK.md reference + rtk-prefix rule to the AGENTS.md spec in Section 1.4"
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk cargo build` — strips "Compiling..." lines, keeps errors/warnings (70-85% savings)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk cargo test` / `rtk cargo nextest` — shows only failures (90%+ savings)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk cargo clippy` — groups warnings by lint rule (60-80% savings)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk cargo check` — strips "Checking..." progress (75-85% savings)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk git status/diff/log` — compact diff algorithm (60-90% savings)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:  PreToolUse -->|"stdin JSON"| rtkRewrite["~/.claude/hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh"]
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:  rtkRewrite -->|"rtk rewrite CMD"| rtkBinary["rtk binary"]
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:  rtkBinary -->|"rewritten CMD"| shell["shell executes rtk git status etc."]
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:`rtk init --global` installs three things into `~/.claude/`:
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh` — thin Bash delegator, SHA-256 integrity verified
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- Commands to use with `rtk` prefix vs. those that pass through
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- `rtk gain` for monitoring savings
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:> When running cargo or git commands, prefer the `rtk` prefix variants (e.g. `rtk cargo build`, `rtk git status`) for token efficiency.
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- [ ] Install RTK: `brew tap rtk-ai/rtk && brew install rtk` (macOS, recommended)
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- [ ] Run `rtk init --global` to install the Claude Code hook
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- [ ] Verify: `rtk --version` and `rtk gain` work
./.cursor/plans/RTK Integration Plan-d7d053c0.plan.md:- [ ] Smoke test: `rtk cargo check` in repo root — confirm filtered output
./.cursor/plans/Hooks Gap Analysis-43c329b6.plan.md:  Installed *globally* into `~/.claude/settings.json` by `rtk init --global`. It rewrites `cargo`/`git` calls to the `rtk` prefix. This is the only Claude Code hook the plans mention — and it is opt-in, developer-machine-only, with no project-level fallback.
./.cursor/rules/agent-guardrails.mdc:Always use `rtk` prefix for cargo/git commands in Cursor terminal:
./.cursor/rules/agent-guardrails.mdc:`rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo clippy`, `rtk cargo nextest run`, `rtk git status`.
./.cursor/rules/agent-guardrails.mdc:Do NOT use `rtk` in Claude Code or Gemini CLI sessions — context-mode handles compression there.
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:3. **RTK-prefixed commands** — `rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo clippy`, etc. Reduces context 60–90%. Always use `rtk` for shell commands in Cursor.
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:Always use `rtk` prefix for cargo/git commands in Cursor's integrated terminal:
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk cargo build
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk cargo clippy -- -D warnings -D clippy::pedantic
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk cargo nextest run
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk cargo fmt --check
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk git status
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:rtk git diff
./.cursor/rules/task-routing.mdc:3. **RTK verify** — `rtk cargo clippy -- -D warnings -D clippy::pedantic && rtk cargo fmt --check`.

./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:**Claude Code**: context-mode's PreToolUse hooks intercept Bash calls and route data-heavy output through sandboxed execution (output never enters context). No `rtk` prefix needed.
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:rtk cargo fmt -- --check && rtk cargo clippy -- -D warnings -D clippy::pedantic
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:| **Cursor** | RTK (`rtk` prefix) | QMD (semantic) | serena | Dynamic | **No** (Simulated only) |
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:| Validation (fmt + clippy + test) | `Bash` with `&&` chain | `rtk cargo fmt && rtk cargo clippy && rtk cargo nextest run` | context-mode auto-handles output; Cursor uses RTK |
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:| Shell commands with large output | `ctx_execute` (automatic via hooks) | `rtk` prefix | context-mode indexes output, returns snippets |
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:    CUR_Q4 -->|Yes| RtkChain["Chain with &&\nusing rtk prefix"]
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:- [ ] **Claude Code**: `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy -- -D warnings -D clippy::pedantic && cargo nextest run` passes (no `rtk` prefix — context-mode handles compression)
./plans/0a-mcp-tool-optimization.md:- [ ] **Cursor**: `rtk cargo fmt --check && rtk cargo clippy -- -D warnings -D clippy::pedantic && rtk cargo nextest run` passes
./plans/0-multi-ide-architecture.md:13. **RTK (token optimization):** When running cargo or git commands in a shell, prefer the `rtk` prefix variants (e.g. `rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo nextest run`, `rtk cargo clippy`, `rtk git status`). This reduces context window usage by 60-90%. Falls back transparently if RTK is not installed.
./plans/0-multi-ide-architecture.md:# RTK: always use rtk prefix (rtk cargo build, rtk cargo clippy, rtk git status) in Cursor terminal.
./plans/0-multi-ide-architecture.md:# 2. RTK-prefixed commands — rtk cargo/git in terminal
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:- [x] Install RTK: `cargo install --git https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk` (brew tap not available; installed v0.29.0).
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:- [x] Run `rtk init --global` to install the Claude Code `PreToolUse` hook.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:- [x] Verify: `rtk --version` works (`rtk 0.29.0`). `~/.cargo/bin` added to PATH in settings.json.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:- [x] Smoke test: `rtk cargo check` — confirmed filtered output (`✓ cargo build (0 crates compiled)`). Hook active.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:RTK installs a `PreToolUse` hook into `~/.claude/settings.json`. When Claude Code issues a Bash command, the hook intercepts it and rewrites it (e.g. `cargo build` → `rtk cargo build`). RTK executes the real command, filters the output, and returns only the signal — errors, warnings, failures — stripping all progress noise.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:  PreToolUse -->|"stdin JSON"| hook["~/.claude/hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh"]
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:  hook -->|"rtk rewrite CMD"| rtk["rtk binary"]
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:  rtk -->|"filtered output"| ClaudeCode
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:Exit codes are always preserved. The hook is SHA-256 integrity-verified. Re-running `rtk init --global` is idempotent.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo build` | "Compiling…" progress lines | 70–85% |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo test` | Passing test output | 90%+ |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo nextest` | Passing test output | 90%+ |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo clippy` | Duplicate warnings (grouped by lint rule) | 60–80% |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo check` | "Checking…" progress lines | 75–85% |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk cargo install` | Dependency compilation noise | 80–90% |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:| `rtk git status/diff/log` | Compact diff algorithm | 60–90% |
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:Unsupported commands pass through unchanged (`rtk cargo fmt`, `rtk cargo run`, etc. are transparent passthroughs that still track metrics).
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:- **No project-level config needed.** RTK is configured globally via `~/.config/rtk/config.toml` (XDG). No repo-level files are required.
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:Once installed, Claude Code reads `~/.claude/RTK.md` (a 10-line slim reference injected by `rtk init`). The project `AGENTS.md` adds an explicit rule directing agents to use the `rtk` prefix for cargo and git commands (see `plans/0-multi-ide-architecture.md` Section 2.4, rule 13).
./plans/1-project-guardrails.md:Use `rtk gain` at any time to inspect token savings by command and project.

./AGENTS.md:Always use `rtk` prefix when available: `rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo clippy`, `rtk git status`.
./AGENTS.md:**Cursor only** — Use `rtk` prefix variants: `rtk cargo build`, `rtk cargo nextest run`, `rtk cargo clippy`, `rtk git status`. Reduces context window usage 60-90%. Falls back transparently if RTK not installed.
./AGENTS.md:**Claude Code** — Do NOT use `rtk` prefix. context-mode handles output compression via sandboxed execution (PreToolUse hooks). RTK's shell rewriting is subsumed by context-mode's intent-driven filtering, which keeps raw output out of context entirely.
./CLAUDE.md:**Important**: Do NOT use `rtk` prefix in Claude Code sessions. context-mode's PreToolUse hooks handle output compression. RTK is for Cursor only.

# Persona: Senior Rust Systems Engineer (Security & Performance Focused).
# See AGENTS.md for full project rules.
@AGENTS.md

## Context Management

- Use `rg` for targeted search. Never read `Cargo.lock` in full.
- Prefer `--count-matches` / `--files-with-matches` for broad searches; drill into files only when needed.
- Summarize outputs > 100 lines before proceeding.

## Parallelism

- Use Codebase Investigator subagent for architecture analysis (experimental).
- Split large work into separate sessions when exceeding context budget.
- Prefer parallel tool calls for independent tasks (no shared files, no ordering dependency).

## Delegation Role

- Act as the research leg of the pipeline: analyze large code areas, summarize architecture, and identify candidate files or risks.
- Prefer read-only investigation over implementation when the task can be answered through explanation or synthesis.
- Once the architecture is clear, hand code-writing work back to Claude Code or Cursor Agent CLI rather than trying to be the primary execution engine.
- When producing recommendations, include likely edit targets, constraints from `AGENTS.md`, and open questions that could block implementation.

## Semantic Search

- Run `qmd search "query"` to find relevant code before reading files.
- Keep index current: `qmd update --quiet && qmd embed --quiet`.

## MCP Sandbox

For tasks requiring 2+ MCP tool calls, use `activtrak-sandbox`:
1. `search_tools(query)` → discover tool names
2. `execute_code(script)` → call tools in a loop, return string summary

Tool naming: `serverName__toolName` (double underscore).

## Escalation Rules

- Tests fail after 3 attempts → stop and report failing test + assertion + full output.
- Blocked or uncertain → ask rather than improvise.
## Git Review & Commit Standards

- **Hyper-Atomicity:** Commit after every logical task or tool execution.
- **Verification Gate:** Run `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy` before every commit.
- **The "Why" Mandate:** Every commit message MUST include a body explaining the intent/reasoning.

**Workflow Hint:**
Use `git diff --cached | gemini -p "Generate a Conventional Commit message. Explain the WHY (intent) based on the code changes."`
